attributes.description: Alerts when `haproxy.connections.errors` increases by more than 10 in 5 minutes. Connection errors indicate backend-reach problems.
attributes.name: [HAProxy OTel] Connection errors spike

investigation_guide.blob:
## HAProxy connection errors spike

### What fired
`haproxy.connections.errors` increased by > 10 in the window.

### Why it matters
Connection errors mean HAProxy failed the TCP connect to a backend server. Usually indicates crashed servers, network problems, or health-check-flapping targets.

### Triage
1. Check health-check failures and per-server downtime.
2. Inspect network between HAProxy and the affected backend.
3. Look at retry/redispatch counters.

### Remediation
- Fix the unreachable server(s) or network path.
- Remove dead targets from the backend.

### Tuning
- Threshold 10 errors in 5m; tune to your traffic baseline.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-haproxyreceiver.otel-*
// Connection errors (counter) — use INCREASE for window delta
| WHERE haproxy.connections.errors IS NOT NULL
| STATS err_increase = SUM(INCREASE(haproxy.connections.errors))
  BY haproxy.proxy_name, haproxy.service_name, haproxy.addr
// Adjust threshold for sensitivity (10 = 10 new errors in window)
| WHERE err_increase > 10
| EVAL alert_key = CONCAT(TO_STRING(haproxy.proxy_name), "::", TO_STRING(haproxy.service_name), "::", COALESCE(TO_STRING(haproxy.addr), ""))
| SORT err_increase DESC
| LIMIT 50
